steps.title: Identify the right triangle formed by the cone's dimensions
steps.content: Point A is the apex of the cone, B is a point on the base circumference, and the center of the base is directly below A. The height of 43 cm (vertical leg), the radius r (horizontal leg), and the slant distance AB = 86 cm (hypotenuse) form a right triangle. We can apply the Pythagorean theorem to find the radius.

steps.title: Use the Pythagorean theorem to find the radius
Calculate the volume using the cone formula
steps.content:
The volume of a cone is $V = \frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$. Substituting $r = 43\sqrt{3}$ and $h = 43$:
$$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi(43\sqrt{3})^2(43)$$
$$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi(43^2 \cdot 3)(43)$$
$$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi \cdot 1849 \cdot 3 \cdot 43$$
$$V = \pi \cdot 1849 \cdot 43$$
$$V = 79507\pi$$

Therefore, $k = 79507$.
